pages.text: Ages ago, when rocks were still soft and trees could talk, all the animals lived on land under one big shed.

blocks.text: Ages ago, when rocks were still soft and trees could talk, all the animals lived on land under one big shed.

pages.text: They took care of each other and protected one another from human trouble all day and night.

blocks.text: They took care of each other and protected one another from human trouble all day and night.

pages.text: At sunset, the elephants trumpeted and the birds chirped along in harmony, while the peacock danced.

blocks.text: At sunset, the elephants trumpeted and the birds chirped along in harmony, while the peacock danced.

pages.text: One night, it was Mr Owl’s turn to guard the animals as they slept.

blocks.text: One night, it was Mr Owl’s turn to guard the animals as they slept.

pages.text: He stayed up watching far and wide for any sign of humans.

blocks.text: He stayed up watching far and wide for any sign of humans.

pages.text: Suddenly the bush in front of him crackled and shook.

blocks.text: Suddenly the bush in front of him crackled and shook.

pages.text: “Aww!” Mr Owl was trapped in the hands of a small boy. Owl hooted and ruffled his feathers. There was even a toot.

blocks.text: “Aww!” Mr Owl was trapped in the hands of a small boy.
Owl hooted and ruffled his feathers. There was even a toot.

pages.text: “Please don’t roast me! Take the other animals. I am too wise to be eaten – I won’t be as delicious.”

blocks.text: “Please don’t roast me! Take the other animals. I am too wise to be eaten – I won’t be as delicious.”

pages.text: The boy shrugged, heading under the animals’ shed. He lit a fire, waking the animals from their sleep. “Fire!” they shouted, as they scattered all over.

blocks.text: The boy shrugged, heading under the animals’ shed. He lit a fire, waking the animals from their sleep.
“Fire!” they shouted, as they scattered all over.

pages.text: The insects hid underground. Some animals ran for the river and never came back. The birds flew deep into the night sky.

blocks.text: Some animals ran for the river and never came back.
The birds flew deep into the night sky.

pages.text: The humans left with the cats, cows and dogs, leaving Mr Owl ashamed. “They must never see me again.”

blocks.text: The humans left with the cats, cows and dogs, leaving Mr Owl ashamed. “They must never see me again.”

pages.text: From that night, he vowed never to show his face in daylight … only at night to eat and stretch his wings.

blocks.text: From that night, he vowed never to show his face in daylight … only at night to eat and stretch his wings.
